author: Sofia Martínez
title: How We Use Claude API to Automate 80% of Our Clients' Marketing Content
description: Claude API lets us generate, review, and publish branded content at scale without losing quality. Here's the exact workflow we run.

sections.p: Content is the bottleneck for most small business marketing programs. The strategy is clear, the channels are set up, but producing enough quality content to keep everything fed — blogs, emails, social, ads — requires time most business owners don't have. Claude API is how we solve that at scale without sacrificing the quality that makes content actually work.

sections.h2: What we automate vs what stays human

sections.p: We automate the production layer: first drafts of blog posts, email sequences, social captions, ad copy variations. We keep human judgment on the strategy layer: what to say, to whom, at what moment. The model handles volume; the strategist handles meaning.

This isn't 'set it and forget it' AI content. Every piece goes through a brand voice check and a human edit pass before it publishes. But that edit pass takes 10 minutes instead of 2 hours, because the heavy lifting is done.

list: Monday: content brief is written with target queries, audience segment, and key message.
Tuesday: Claude API generates 3 draft variations per content piece.
Wednesday: strategist reviews, selects, and edits the best variation.
Thursday–Friday: approved content is scheduled across channels.

sections.p: We tested several LLM APIs before standardizing on Claude. The difference that mattered for marketing content: Claude follows detailed system prompts reliably. When we tell it to write in a specific brand voice, target a specific persona, and avoid certain phrases, it actually does — consistently across hundreds of outputs.

For clients with strong brand guidelines, that consistency is the difference between AI content that sounds like them and AI content that sounds like everyone else's AI content. Anthropic's focus on instruction-following made Claude the practical choice.

sections.h2: What this means for client costs

sections.p: Before AI-assisted production, a full content program — 4 blogs, 8 emails, 20 social posts per month — cost clients $2,500–$4,000/month in content creation fees. With our Claude-powered workflow, we deliver the same volume at a fraction of that cost, and we reinvest the savings into distribution and optimization instead.

sections.quote: The goal isn't to replace writers. It's to let one good writer do the work of five, and focus their judgment where it actually matters.
